title: Mount Maunganui Budget Guide
meta_desc: Visit Tauranga and Mount Maunganui on a family budget of $180-380/day, with free activities, affordable accommodation, and tips to save money and splurge on the best experiences
intro: Tauranga and Mount Maunganui are a classic Kiwi summer holiday destination, offering a range of activities and experiences for families. With some planning, you can have a great time on a budget of $180-380 per day for a family of four. From free beach activities to affordable accommodation options, we'll show you how to make the most of your trip.

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body:
Mount Maunganui has a range of accommodation options to suit all budgets. For a cheap option, consider a campsite or holiday park, such as the Mount Maunganui Beachside Holiday Resort, which offers powered sites from $40 per night. Mid-range options include apartments and motels, like the Ocean Waves Motel, which costs around $180 per night for a family room.

For a more luxurious experience, you can opt for a beachfront apartment or house, which can cost upwards of $400 per night. However, these often come with extra amenities like full kitchens and laundry facilities, which can help you save money in the long run.
list: Campsite or holiday park: $40-60 per night
Mid-range apartment or motel: $180-250 per night
Luxury beachfront apartment or house: $400-600 per night

sections.heading: Free Activities
sections.body:
One of the best things about Mount Maunganui is the range of free activities on offer. The beach is patrolled by lifeguards during the summer months, making it a safe and enjoyable spot for families. You can also take a walk to the summit of Mount Maunganui, which offers stunning views of the Bay of Plenty.

Papamoa Hills Regional Park is another great spot for a free family outing, with walks and views over the bay. Pilot Bay on the harbour side of the Mount is a hidden gem, perfect for toddlers learning to swim.
list: Mount Beach: patrolled by lifeguards during summer
Walk to the summit of Mount Maunganui: 1-1.5 hours return
Papamoa Hills Regional Park: walks and views over the bay

sections.heading: Budget Breakdown
sections.body: To help you plan your trip, we've put together a rough 3-day budget breakdown for a family of four. This assumes you'll be staying in mid-range accommodation and eating a mix of self-catered and restaurant meals.

sections.heading: Tips to Save Money
sections.body:
To save money on your trip to Mount Maunganui, consider self-catering for some meals, rather than eating out for every meal. You can also look for discounts and deals on activities and accommodation, especially if you're booking in advance.

Another way to save money is to take advantage of the free activities on offer, such as the beach and walks in the regional park. By being mindful of your spending and making a few smart choices, you can have a great family holiday on a budget of $180-380 per day.
